sources.author: Swyx
sources.headline: Developer at Temporal, AI Researcher
sources.post_summary: Discusses the evolution of AI agents from hype to practical deployment, emphasizing multi-agent systems and their integration with developer workflows. Shares insights on challenges in reliability and scaling AI agents for production use. Highlights upcoming tools for agent orchestration.

sources.author: Andrej Karpathy
sources.headline: Founder at Eureka Labs, ex-OpenAI
sources.post_summary: Explores how AI agents are transforming software development by automating code generation and debugging. Predicts a shift where developers focus more on architecture and less on boilerplate. References recent benchmarks showing agent efficiency gains.

sources.author: Nat Friedman
sources.headline: Co-founder at GitHub, Investor
sources.post_summary: Analyzes the AI infrastructure stack needed for scalable agent deployment, including vector databases and orchestration layers. Points out gaps in current tools and recommends open-source alternatives. Ties it to recent enterprise adoptions.

sources.author: Guillermo Rauch
sources.headline: CEO at Vercel
sources.post_summary: Shares updates on Vercel's AI SDK for building agentic applications with Next.js. Demonstrates how it simplifies deployment of AI-powered automations for developers. Includes code snippets and performance metrics from beta users.

sources.author: Addy Osmani
sources.headline: Engineering Leader at Google Chrome
sources.post_summary: Dives into browser-based AI agents for web automation, covering WebGPU acceleration and privacy considerations. Outlines developer tools for prototyping agents directly in the browser. Discusses real-world use cases in productivity apps.

sources.author: Sami Rahman
sources.headline: Founder @ RevGenius | GTM Engineering Expert
sources.post_summary: Discusses building scalable GTM engines by integrating engineering with sales processes. Shares a framework for automating outbound at scale while maintaining personalization. Emphasizes data-driven iteration for revenue growth.

sources.author: Kyle Coleman
sources.headline: VP Sales Ops @ Outreach | Revenue Operations Leader
sources.post_summary: Outlines how sales ops teams can leverage automation tools to optimize outbound campaigns. Provides case study on 3x lead response rates through integrated tech stacks. Advises on common pitfalls in scaling sales ops.

sources.author: Ankur Nagpal
sources.headline: CEO @ Teachable | Content & GTM Strategist
sources.post_summary: Explains a content strategy playbook for B2B GTM that drives inbound leads and supports outbound efforts. Details content flywheels integrating SEO, repurposing, and sales enablement. Shares metrics from his experience scaling to $100M ARR.

sources.author: Jacqueline Stone
sources.headline: Head of RevOps @ Gong | Sales Operations Specialist
sources.post_summary: Breaks down revenue operations as the glue between sales, marketing, and product. Focuses on outbound automation workflows that align with customer data platforms. Includes actionable steps for sales ops to implement immediately.

sources.author: Dan Tyre
sources.headline: Outbound Sales Expert | HubSpot Alum
sources.post_summary: Shares battle-tested outbound automation strategies that blend cold email sequences with LinkedIn touchpoints. Highlights personalization at scale using AI tools and A/B testing for GTM success. Warns against over-automation pitfalls.

sources.author: Pieter Levels
sources.headline: Indie Hacker & Founder of Nomad List, Remote OK
sources.post_summary: Pieter shares his journey as a solo developer shipping multiple products like Nomad List and Remote OK without funding. He emphasizes bootstrapping, rapid iteration, and building in public to validate ideas quickly. His post details revenue milestones and lessons on focusing on user feedback over perfection.

sources.author: Arvid Kahl
sources.headline: Founder of FeedbackPanda (acq. by Podia), Author of The Embedded Entrepreneur
sources.post_summary: Arvid recounts pivoting from corporate life to solo founding FeedbackPanda, which he bootstrapped to $300k ARR before selling. He discusses building in public, audience-first product development, and the power of niche markets for solo devs. Key takeaway: Start small, ship fast, and leverage your network.

sources.author: KP (Karthik Pattabiraman)
sources.headline: Solo Founder & Developer at Superhuman-like tools
sources.post_summary: KP documents his 100-day challenge building and shipping AI-powered tools as a solo dev, sharing weekly updates on progress, failures, and launches. He highlights tools like email summarizers hitting 1k users organically. Advice: Consistency in shipping beats big launches.

sources.author: Sahil Lavingia
sources.headline: Founder & CEO at Gumroad
sources.post_summary: Sahil shares his career pivot from Pinterest to founding Gumroad as a solo project that scaled to millions in revenue. He advocates building in public for accountability and community support during pivots into tech entrepreneurship. Post includes tips on minimal viable products and creator economy.

sources.author: Daniel Vassallo
sources.headline: Ex-AWS, Indie Maker & Small Bets Advocate
sources.post_summary: Daniel details his pivot from Amazon engineer to solo maker, shipping micro-SaaS products generating $10k+/mo. He stresses small bets, building in public, and avoiding VC for sustainable solo development. Shares metrics and experiments for aspiring pivots.
